summary: Large decompilation/matching PR described by the author as "Mostly kirby and crazy hand," with additional item, stage, game-mode, toy, and header cleanups. The match report credited 81 new matches and +14,452 matched code bytes, moving matched code to 51.67% (+0.37%). The biggest bodies of work were Crazy Hand init/action routines, Kirby init/special cleanup, Koopa shell item routines, and several small item reflected/destroyed/collision handlers. Review focused on moving reusable sub-stick accessors into baselib/controller.h and on future item function renames away from unsplit-file names like it_3F14_Logic17_Destroyed.
what_changed: Replaced many placeholder stubs with matching C implementations across 62 files, with the match bot reporting 81 new matches and +14,452 matched code bytes.
Added a large set of Crazy Hand routines in ftCh_Init.c, including wait/walk/drill/rock-crush/finger-beam/back-airplane/tag-grab/tag-squeeze/grab-related routines and supporting prototypes/includes.

Adjusted Crazy Hand motion-variable usage in the diff excerpt from fp->mv.mh fields to fp->mv.ch fields in several places, indicating cleanup of copied Master Hand-style code into Crazy Hand-specific storage.
Added local matching helpers in Crazy Hand code, including an inline my_sqrtf using __frsqrte and my_lbVector_Len, plus a temporary get_jobj helper with a todo about eventually using GET_JOBJ.
Implemented several ftCommon Attack100/catch/capture-jump helpers, including ftCo_Catch_CheckInput, fn_800D86B8, fn_800DA4C0, and ftCo_CaptureJump_IASA.
Exposed ftCo_800951D0 outside ftCo_ItemThrow.c by changing it from static to non-static and adding its declaration to ftCo_ItemThrow.h.
Updated a throw prototype from fn_80105FEC(void) to fn_80105FEC(HSD_GObj* gobj), improving header accuracy.

Added or adjusted many item handlers for shell items and reflected/destroyed logic in files such as itzgshell.c, itrshell.c, itfreeze.c, itlipstickspore.c, itgamewatchchef.c, ittools.c, itnesspkflush.c, and others.
Matched two toy input helper routines, un_80305EB4 and un_80305FB8, and after review moved normalized sub-stick accessor helpers into sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h.
Included smaller stage/game-mode/data-section improvements in grvenom, groldkongo, grpushon, gm_18A5, gm_1832, and related types headers.
smart_moves: Used existing engine helpers such as Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ftBossLib movement helpers, lbVector_Diff, and lbVector_Normalize rather than reimplementing high-level behavior wholesale.

For matching-sensitive float/vector code, introduced a local my_sqrtf based on __frsqrte refinement and a my_lbVector_Len wrapper, which likely helped reproduce compiler output where the generic library helper did not match exactly.

Preserved exact matching details where needed, including PAD_STACK(8), #pragma dont_inline around ftCo_CaptureJump_IASA, explicit 1.0f/0.0f/NULL arguments, and small dummy stack arrays in matching helpers.
Promoted a previously static helper, ftCo_800951D0, only when another matched function needed to call it, and mirrored the change with a header declaration.
Accepted review feedback to centralize normalized sub-stick accessors in baselib/controller.h instead of leaving file-local copies in toy.c, improving reuse and avoiding duplicate inline accessors.
Deferred broad naming cleanup into a follow-up rather than mixing mass item renames into an already large matching PR, reducing risk from rebase churn and review noise.
decomp_lessons: When matching boss character clones or closely related code, verify motion-variable namespaces carefully; Crazy Hand code in this PR required using fp->mv.ch rather than fp->mv.mh in several copied-looking regions.
Tiny ABI/prototype corrections in headers can unblock matches elsewhere; examples include adding an HSD_GObj* parameter to fn_80105FEC and exposing ftCo_800951D0.
If a helper is broadly useful and corresponds to baselib/controller data access, prefer adding a named inline in sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h rather than creating one-off local accessors.

For item files that were split out of larger units, old names like it_3F14_Logic17_Destroyed are acknowledged as holdovers; future work should prefer file-scoped names such as itFreeze_Destroyed when renaming is feasible.
Large AI-assisted duplicate-code PRs can deliver substantial matching gains but should be followed by naming and cleanup passes, especially for generated or inherited symbol names.
Matching floating-point vector length code may require reproducing the original reciprocal-square-root refinement sequence instead of calling a cleaner math helper.
naming_conventions: Reviewer guidance: item functions should eventually use file-name-based prefixes, e.g. itFreeze_Destroyed, instead of unsplit-file holdover names like it_3F14_Logic17_Destroyed.

Crazy Hand functions in this PR continue the ftCh_ prefix convention for named action callbacks, with address-suffixed names retained where semantics are still unknown, such as ftCh_GrabUnk1_8015BA34.
Common fighter routines retain ftCo_ prefixes; unknown functions keep fn_ address names such as fn_800D86B8 and fn_800DA4C0 until better names are known.
Shell item functions use item-specific prefixes in matched names shown by the report, such as itRshell_UnkMotion7_Coll and itZrshell_UnkMotion9_Coll.
Reusable controller accessors should be named under the HSD_PadGet... style, as with HSD_PadGetNmlSubStickX and HSD_PadGetNmlSubStickY.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline on/reset around ftCo_CaptureJump_IASA to preserve expected call/inlining behavior.
Inserted PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_CaptureJump_IASA, indicating stack-layout padding was necessary for a match.
Used an inline reciprocal square root sequence with __frsqrte and three Newton-style refinement steps in my_sqrtf to match sqrtf-like codegen.
Added small unused stack arrays such as u8 _[4] and u8 _[8] in matching-sensitive functions to force stack shape/register allocation.
Kept exact float literals and NULL pointer arguments in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, replacing integer 1/0/0 with 1.0f/0.0f/NULL in some places.
Used RETURN_IF macros in ftCo_CaptureJump_IASA to match early-return structure in input-check chains.
Created static forward declarations inside C files when matched functions needed to call later local routines, as seen with fn_800D84D4 and several Crazy Hand helpers.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Move HSD_PadGetNmlSubStickX/Y-style helpers into sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h because a normal-stick helper already exists there and reuse keeps things cleaner.
review_feedback.outcome: Author replied "done"; changed files include src/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h with five added lines.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether Claude-generated functions could be renamed to match the file-name format; example given was itFreeze_Destroyed instead of it_3F14_Logic17_Destroyed because the it_3F14 style is a holdover from unsplit files.
review_feedback.outcome: Author replied that a mass renaming would be done in a follow-up PR if acceptable.

review_feedback.author: lukechampine
review_feedback.feedback: Noted conflicts were fixed and apologized for rebase churn.

follow_up_queries: Find the follow-up PR that mass-renamed item functions from it_3F14/it_2725 style to file-prefix names such as itFreeze_Destroyed.
Search for later cleanup replacing ftCh_Init.c local my_sqrtf/my_lbVector_Len with a shared lbVector or math helper while preserving matches.
Check whether the ftCh_Init.c todo about using GET_JOBJ instead of get_jobj was resolved.
Review subsequent PRs touching Crazy Hand mv.ch vs mv.mh fields for struct/type refinements.
Search for additional HSD_PadGetNmlSubStickX/Y users after the controller.h move.
